solution.p: To add two fractions with different denominators, we first have to find the common denominator.
In this case, the least common multiple of 9 and 18 is 18.
Look at the first fraction \displaystyle{\frac{5}{9}}. To change the denominator to 18, we multiply 2 at both the top and bottom, and we have:
The second fraction already has the common denominator.
Finally, we can do the addition. The full process is:
